  # Full rubric pipeline integration in benchmark and side-by-side cost/latency report                                
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sun, 28 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching

  pieces/style-pieces.service.ts . Map selected candidates onto the existing response shape for backwards-            
  compatibility,                                                                                                      
  and add new optional response fields consumed by the frontend.

  • outfits[].why ← rationale.concept_alignment (≤180 chars, back-compat)

  the frontend can show "styled for analogous warm palette, A-line silhouette, broad-shoulder balance"                
                                                                                                                      
  Update frontend TypeScript types for rationale and plan_summary . The /style-pieces/regenerate path also runs       
  through                                                                                                             
  SelectionService (picks 1 instead of 3; diversity constraint relaxes to hero piece differing from prior_outfit ).   
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • /style-pieces response assembled via SelectionService , not direct Sonnet post-processing                         
  • outfits[].why populated from new path (back-compat with current callers)                                          
  • plan_summary field present on response (optional, may be null if plan unavailable)                                
  • Frontend TypeScript types include rationale and plan_summary fields                                               
  • Existing /style-pieces e2e tests pass with the new assembly path                                                  
  • /style-pieces/regenerate wired to SelectionService with relaxed diversity constraint

⭐ Subtask  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Mon, 29 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-99  💭 0 comments  🧵 3 linked                       
                                                                                                                      
  # StylingCriticService Haiku scoring — texture, focal-point, and threshold filter                                   
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sun, 28 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Extend backend/src/styling-critic/styling-critic.service.ts (scaffold from companion sub-task) with Haiku-based     
  texture                                                                                                             
  scoring and focal-point audit. Run all K candidates in parallel via Promise.all , reusing ToolRunner with the Haiku 
  model.                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Texture scoring** (rubric Step 4): one Haiku call per candidate reading item descriptions → returns 4 sub-        
  dimension                                                                                                           
  scores: surface_contrast (0–40), pattern_discipline (0–30), weight_coherence (0–20), seasonal_hand (0–10); total =  
  texture_score (0–100).                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Focal-point audit** (rubric Step 5): one Haiku call per candidate counting independent focal elements →           
  focal_point_count + focal_points[] with reasons.                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  **Threshold filter** : reject if texture_score ❬ 60 (or ❬ 50 when concept_authority === HIGH ); reject if           
  focal_point_count ≠ 1 (allow 2 when override_flag === true and concept brief calls for focal tension).              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • One Haiku call per candidate for texture, one for focal-point (not serialised — confirmed via Promise.all )       
  • texture_dimensions with all 4 sub-scores populated on each scored candidate                                       
  • focal_points[] with reasons populated                                                                             
  • Threshold filter drops failing candidates and fills reject_reasons[]                                              
  • Editorial override: concept_authority === HIGH lowers texture threshold; override_flag allows focal_point_count   
  === 2                                                                                                               
  • Integration tests mock Haiku tool responses for texture + focal scoring

  Replace the flat "compose N outfits" prompt in backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts with a structured

  • silhouette_rules , palette_rules , concept_authority are injected as explicit prompt constraints

  • Internal candidate count is a configurable constant defaulting to K=6                                             
  • K=6 candidates returned to the caller (KAN-80 trims to 3 for the response)                                        
  • Unit tests in backend/test/unit/ cover prompt construction from StylingPlan fixtures

⭐ Subtask  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sun, 28 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-97  💭 0 comments  🧵 1 linked                       
                                                                                                                      
  # Benchmark script skeleton, fixture set, and baseline-only mode                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sun, 28 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Create the benchmark script skeleton and fixture set. This sub-task can ship before the rubric pipeline (KAN-77 →   
  KAN-                                                                                                                
  80) is complete because --baseline-only mode runs only the existing single-Sonnet /style-pieces pipeline.           
                                                                                                                      
  **Script** : backend/scripts/styling-benchmark.ts (match existing scripts/ conventions — ts-node or compiled).      
  Register as bench:styling in backend/package.json : npm run --prefix backend bench:styling [-- --fixtures=path --   
  output=path --                                                                                                      
  baseline-only] .                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  **Fixtures** ( backend/test/fixtures/styling/ ): 10 representative cases with shape {{{ name, user_profile, vibe,   
  occasion, items, expected_signals? }}}, providing diversity across 5 body shapes × 3 occasions (editorial /         
  commercial / personal) × 3 vibe families (minimalist / maximalist / streetwear).

  # SelectionService — composite scoring, diversity constraint, and fallback path                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sun, 28 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Create SelectionService as a new module at backend/src/selection/selection.service.ts (following the                
  backend/src/styling-critic/ pattern — a pipeline-internal service with no controller). It picks the final primary   
  look                                                                                                                
  + 2 alternates from the K=6 scored candidates produced by StylingCriticService (KAN-99).                            
                                                                                                                      
  **Algorithm** :                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  1. Drop candidates where passes_filter === false                                                                    
  2. Fallback if 0 survive: return highest-scoring rejected candidate with fallback_flag in rationale                 
  3. Rank survivors by composite score: texture_score × 0.5 + focal_point_cleanliness × 0.3 + (1 − hierarchy_conflicts.
  length) × 0.2 (weights as configurable constants)                                                                   
  4. Pick top-scored as primary                                                                                       
  5. For each alternate: pick highest-scoring survivor whose hero piece differs from primary and from prior alternates
  6. If fewer than 2 hero-distinct candidates remain, set alternates_flag: "Insufficient piece variety to generate    
  distinct alternates"                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Composite scoring weights are configurable constants (not magic numbers)                                          
  • Always returns the requested count when ≥ count candidates survive filter                                         
  • Alternates each have a hero piece distinct from primary and from each other (unit-tested)                         
  • Fallback path (all 6 candidates rejected) returns highest-scoring rejected candidate + fallback_flag              
  • alternates_flag set when fewer than 2 hero-distinct candidates available                                          
  • Unit tests in backend/test/unit/ cover primary selection, diversity constraint, fallback, and insufficient-variety
  paths

⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sun, 28 Jun 26  👷 Jae Yoon Bae  🔑️ KAN-81  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                        
                                                                                                                      
  # Styling benchmark harness — fixture-driven side-by-side vs baseline                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Fixture-driven benchmark harness that runs the new rubric pipeline (KAN-76 → KAN-77 → KAN-79 → KAN-80) against the  
  current single-Sonnet baseline on the same inputs and emits a side-by-side report. Used to validate quality lift    
  before                                                                                                              
  shipping and as a regression net after.h2. Scope                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  **Script location**                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  • backend/scripts/styling-benchmark.ts (mjs/ts — match existing scripts/)                                           
  • npm script: bench:styling in backend/package.json                                                                 
  • Run: npm run --prefix backend bench:styling [-- --fixtures=path --output=path]                                    
                                                                                                                      
  **Fixture format (backend/test/fixtures/styling/)**                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  • 10 representative cases at MVP, expandable                                                                        
  • Each fixture: { name, user_profile, vibe, occasion, items, expected_signals? }                                    
  • Diversity across: 5 body shapes × 3 occasions (editorial / commercial / personal) × 3 vibe families (minimalist / 
  maximalist / streetwear)                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  **What the benchmark runs**                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  For each fixture:                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  1. Run baseline (current single-Sonnet /style-pieces)                                                               
  2. Run rubric pipeline (Plan → Compose K=6 → Critique → Select 3)                                                   
  3. Score both outputs along deterministic rubric checks:                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  • has_wearable_core (top+bottom OR dress)                                                                           
  • hero_role_assigned                                                                                                
  • hero_in_top_or_dress_category                                                                                     
  • volume_rule_respected (against StylingPlan.volume_zone)                                                           
  • anchor_neutral_present_in_outfit                                                                                  
  • texture_score_meets_threshold (where available)                                                                   
  • focal_point_count_equals_1 (where available)                                                                      
  • rationale_per_piece_complete (every item has satisfies field)                                                     
  • alternates_have_distinct_hero_pieces                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  4. Token + latency:                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  • tokens spent per stage (plan, compose, critic, select)                                                            
  • wall-clock per stage                                                                                              
  • total cost in USD using the Anthropic pricing table                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  **Report output**                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  • /tmp/styling-benchmark-/report.md with:                                                                           
  • Per-fixture diff: baseline outfit IDs vs rubric outfit IDs                                                        
  • Rubric compliance table (baseline % vs rubric %)

  • wall-clock per stage

  # Selection + diversity ranking — pick primary + 2 alternates from scored candidates                                
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Pick the final primary look + 2 alternates from the K=6 scored candidates emerging from KAN-79, enforcing the       
  rubric's "meaningfully different premise" diversity rule. Wire the result into the existing /style-pieces response  
  shape (backwards-compatible).h2. Scope                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Input**                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  • K scored candidates from KAN-79 (each with texture_score, focal_point_count, hierarchy_conflicts, roles,          
  rationale)                                                                                                          
  • StylingPlan for context (palette_type, silhouette decision)                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  **Selection algorithm**                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  1. Drop candidates with passes_filter === false                                                                     
  2. If 0 survive: fall back per rubric — re-prompt the composer with reject_reasons, OR (cheaper) return the highest-
  scoring rejected candidate with a flag in rationale                                                                 
  3. Rank survivors by composite score: texture_score weighted 0.5, focal-point cleanliness 0.3, hierarchy_conflicts. 
  length 0.2                                                                                                          
  4. Pick the top-scored candidate as primary                                                                         
  5. For each of the 2 alternates: scan remaining survivors for the highest score whose hero piece differs from the   
  primary AND from previously picked alternates (per rubric Step 8)                                                   
  6. If fewer than 2 hero-distinct candidates remain, surface alternates_flag: "Insufficient piece variety to generate
  distinct alternates" (per Step 8)                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  **Output → /style-pieces response shape**                                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  • outfits: 3 ResolvedOutfit entries with full structured rationale (new field) AND why populated from rationale.    
  concept_alignment (back-compat)                                                                                     
  • New optional field on response: plan_summary — surface the StylingPlan decisions so the frontend can show "styled 
  for analogous warm palette, A-line silhouette, broad-shoulder balance"                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Regenerate path**                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  • /style-pieces/regenerate inherits the same pipeline: PlanService → ComposerService(K=6) → CriticService →         
  SelectionService picks 1 instead of 3                                                                               
  • Diversity constraint relaxes to "hero piece differs from prior_outfit"                                            
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Always returns exactly the requested count when ≥ count candidates survive                                        
  • Alternates have distinct hero pieces from primary (unit-tested)                                                   
  • Composite scoring is configurable via constants                                                                   
  • Fallback path (all reject) tested                                                                                 
  • Existing /style-pieces e2e tests pass with new shape                                                              
  • Frontend types updated for new rationale + plan_summary fields                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Depends on                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  • KAN-79 (critic producing scored candidates)                                                                       
  • KAN-76, KAN-77                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## References                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  • second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling/step-8-alternates.md                                                         
  • second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling/step-7-rationale-output.md

  # StylingCriticService — texture, focal-point, and hierarchy scoring per candidate                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  New backend/src/styling-critic/ module that scores each of the K=6 candidates from KAN-77 along the rubric's quality
  dimensions and applies the deterministic threshold filters. Runs in parallel across candidates.h2. Scope            
                                                                                                                      
  **Inputs (per candidate)**                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • Resolved outfit items (with their catalog description field — already rich Sonnet-generated silhouette/fabric     
  prose)                                                                                                              
  • Assigned roles { hero, supporting, accessory } from KAN-77                                                        
  • StylingPlan from KAN-76 (for editorial override context)                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  **Outputs (per candidate)**                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  • texture_score (0–100)                                                                                             
  • texture_dimensions: surface_contrast (0–40), pattern_discipline (0–30), weight_coherence (0–20), seasonal_hand    
  (0–10)                                                                                                              
  • focal_point_count + focal_points[] with reasons                                                                   
  • hierarchy_conflicts[]                                                                                             
  • passes_filter + reject_reasons[]                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  **Implementation**                                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  • Texture scoring: one Haiku call per candidate that reads the item descriptions and returns the 4 dimension scores 
  per rubric Step 4                                                                                                   
  • Focal-point audit: one Haiku call per candidate counting independent focal elements per rubric Step 5             
  • Hierarchy check: pure-TS deterministic against roles (hero category, supporting non-competing, accessory          
  subordinate) per Step 6                                                                                             
  • All K candidates scored in parallel (Promise.all)                                                                 
  • Reuse ToolRunner; Haiku model                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  **Threshold filter**                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  • texture_score ❬ 60 → reject (or ❬ 50 if concept_authority === HIGH)                                               
  • focal_point_count ≠ 1 → reject (or = 2 allowed if override_flag === true and concept brief calls for focal        
  tension)                                                                                                            
  • hierarchy_conflicts.length ❭ 0 → reject                                                                           
  • All rejects surface a reject_reason; the selector (KAN-80) decides what to do if all 6 reject                     
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • New module at backend/src/styling-critic/ with service + types                                                    
  • Unit tests for hierarchy deterministic checks                                                                     
  • Integration-style tests that mock Haiku responses for texture + focal scoring                                     
  • Editorial override pass-through tested (HIGH authority allows lower texture threshold + focal=2)                  
  • Parallel execution confirmed (no serial Haiku calls)                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Depends on                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  • KAN-77 (composer producing K=6 candidates with roles)                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  ## References                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  • second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling/step-4-texture-scoring.md

  # Rubric composer rewrite — /style-pieces prompt + structured rationale schema                                      
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Sat, 27 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Restructure backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts so it consumes a StylingPlan (from KAN-76) and returns 
  per-                                                                                                                
  piece structured rationale + assigned roles. Overgenerate K=6 candidates internally so the critic (KAN-79) and

  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Replace the single-Sonnet flat prompt in /style-pieces with a structured Plan → Compose → Critique → Select pipeline
  that applies the 9-step styling rubric documented in second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling .h2. Why                  
                                                                                                                      
  The current composer ( backend/src/style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts ) is one Claude Sonnet call with a flat vibe +
  occasion + items prompt. It has no body-shape awareness, no explicit palette architecture, no focal-point or texture
  scoring, and only a 180-char rationale. The rubric formalises how a trained stylist composes: body data → silhouette
  rules, vibe + undertone → palette architecture, occasion → concept authority, then per-candidate scoring on texture 
  and focal point, then per-piece structured rationale.                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Implementation tickets

  --------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------  
   KAN-76                                                 | StylingPlanService — deterministic rules engine for       
                                                          | silhouette, palette, concept authority                    
   KAN-77                                                 | Rubric composer rewrite — /style-pieces prompt +          
                                                          | structured rationale schema                               
   KAN-79                                                 | StylingCriticService — texture, focal-point, and          
                                                          | hierarchy scoring per candidate                           
   KAN-80                                                 | Selection + diversity ranking — pick primary + 2          
                                                          | alternates from scored candidates                         
   KAN-81                                                 | Styling benchmark harness — fixture-driven side-by-side   
                                                          | vs baseline                                               
   Ship order: KAN-76 → KAN-77 → KAN-79 → KAN-80 → KAN-   |                                                           
   81. KAN-81 can run in --baseline-only mode before the  |                                                           
   others land.                                           |                                                           
                                                                                                                      
  ## Rubric reference                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  Full pipeline + rationale at second-brain/fashion-ai/wiki/styling/agent-pipeline.md . Steps 0–8, each with required 
  inputs and produced outputs.                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • All child implementation tickets (KAN-77, KAN-79, KAN-80, KAN-81) shipped and merged                              
  • /style-pieces endpoint runs the full Plan → Compose (K=6) → Critique → Select pipeline end-to-end                 
  • Benchmark (KAN-81) committed with rubric compliance scores for the new pipeline vs single-Sonnet baseline

⭐ Task  🚧 In Progress  ⌛ Sat, 27 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-74  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                   
                                                                                                                      
  # Retrieval bake-off in find-pieces: current vs HyDE vs HyDE+rerank                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 26 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Why                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  Stage 2 ( find-pieces ) is the floor on outfit quality — Stage 3 styling can only ever be as good as the candidates 
  it gets. Today the retrieval query is sparse ( anchor + aesthetic_tags + color_palette[].name ) and embedded once   
  with text-embedding-3-small , then a per-category pgvector cosine search. The query distribution is "tags + color   
  words"; the catalog embeddings are dense product descriptions generated by Sonnet at ingest. That mismatch likely   
  costs us a lot of relevance.                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  Before investing in productionizing any one fix, we want to prove which retrieval strategy actually wins on our     
  catalog and our vibes.                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## What — three strategies in parallel                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  Implement A / B / C side-by-side behind a flag in backend/src/find-pieces/ :                                        
                                                                                                                      
  • **A (control):** current behavior — query string from anchor + tags + color names, one embedding, one cosine      
  search per category.                                                                                                
  • **B (HyDE):** for each category (top/bottom/dress/shoes/accessory), a small Sonnet/Haiku call writes the ideal    
  product description in the same prose voice as catalog descriptions (e.g. "oversized cropped wool blazer in oat,    
  structured shoulder, hip-length, mother-of-pearl buttons"). Embed that, search.                                     
  • **C (HyDE + rerank):** same as B, but pull top 30 per category from the vector search and run a Haiku reranker    
  pass that scores each candidate against the full VibeProfile and returns the top 5 with a one-line rationale.       
  All three strategies run against the same CatalogSearchService and the same gender/price filters — only the query   
  construction (and optional rerank step) differs.                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Eval setup                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  • Pick ~20 representative vibes covering the kinds of inputs we've seen (mix of celebrity-only, celebrity+photo,    
  different aesthetics, both genders).                                                                                
  • For each vibe, capture the top 5 items per category from each of A / B / C.                                       
  • Score two ways:                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  1. **LLM judge:** a Claude Sonnet call sees the VibeProfile and the 5 items (descriptions + image URLs); returns a  
  1–10 score per category + a one-line rationale + a flagged-issue list. Run per (vibe × strategy × category).        
  2. **Human spot-check:** eyeball ~5 of the 20 vibes end-to-end to sanity-check that the judge isn't gaming or       
  systematically biased.                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Aggregate: mean judge score per strategy, per-category breakdown, and win-rate (which strategy wins per vibe).    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Deliverable                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  • The three strategies behind a flag in find-pieces (env var or request-level param — engineer's call, but it must  
  not change default user-facing behavior).                                                                           
  • A small report committed to the repo (markdown or a static HTML page under backend/data/ or similar) with: per-   
  vibe winners, aggregate scores, per-category breakdown, and 3–5 example side-by-side comparisons (vibe → A items vs 
  B items vs C items).                                                                                                
  • A short written recommendation: which strategy to productionize, and what the next retrieval ticket should be.    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Out of scope                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  • Image-side / CLIP retrieval (separate follow-up if B or C wins decisively).                                       
  • Outfit-aware retrieval (picking bottoms conditioned on the chosen top) — separate follow-up.                      
  • Multi-query expansion beyond the single HyDE description per category — separate follow-up.                       
  • Changing the default user-facing retrieval path. This ticket only adds the experimental code paths and produces   
  the report.                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Strategies A, B, and C implemented in backend/src/find-pieces/ behind a flag; default user-facing behavior        
  unchanged                                                                                                           
  • All three strategies query the same CatalogSearchService with the same gender/price filters                       
  • Eval runs against ~20 representative vibes with LLM judge scores (1–10 per strategy per category)                 
  • Report committed under backend/data/ (or similar) in markdown; includes per-vibe winners, aggregate scores, per-  
  category breakdown, and ≥3 side-by-side comparisons                                                                 
  • Written recommendation committed with the report identifying which strategy to productionize and suggesting the   
  next retrieval ticket

  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  frontend, sprint-1  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Update frontend/src/screens/Onboarding/BrandSelection.jsx so brands are rendered under fashion-category headers (e.g.
  Streetwear, Y2K, Old money, Minimal) instead of a flat alphabetical list.                                           
                                                                                                                      
  The brand list comes from GET /brands (backed by backend/src/brands/brands.service.ts which reads distinct brands   
  from catalog_items ). Category membership must be defined in a new static config file — e.g.                        
  frontend/src/screens/Onboarding/brand-categories.js — since the catalog DB has no fashion-category column on brands.
                                                                                                                      
  User selections still persist via updateMe({ preferred_brands: [...] }) to User.preferred_brands (already a String[]
  column on the users table).                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Brands are rendered under labelled category headers; users can select/deselect any brand across all categories    
  • Selections save correctly via updateMe and are returned in subsequent GET /me responses                           
  • Category membership is defined in a clearly named static config file, not inline in JSX                           
  • npm run build (in frontend/ ) passes                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Which brands from the catalog belong to which fashion category (Streetwear, Y2K, Old money, Minimal, etc.)? A   
  suggested mapping from the product owner is needed to seed the static config — or should the developer make a best- 
  effort grouping and get sign-off in the PR review?

Add retrieval strategies A, B (HyDE), and C (HyDE + rerank) side-by-side behind a feature flag in `backend/src/find-pieces/`. All three strategies use the same `CatalogSearchService` with the same gender/price filters — only the query construction (and optional rerank step) differs.

**Strategy A (control):** existing behavior — query string from anchor + tags + color names, one `text-embedding-3-small` embedding, one cosine search per category. Wrap behind the flag without changing default behavior.

**Strategy B (HyDE):** for each category (top/bottom/dress/shoes/accessory), one Haiku/Sonnet call writes an ideal product description in catalog prose voice (e.g. "oversized cropped wool blazer in oat, structured shoulder, hip-length, mother-of-pearl buttons"). Embed that description and run the cosine search.

**Strategy C (HyDE + rerank):** same as B, but pull top 30 per category from the vector search, then run a Haiku reranker pass that scores each candidate against the full VibeProfile and returns the top 5 with a one-line rationale.

## Acceptance criteria

- Strategy A/B/C all implemented in `backend/src/find-pieces/` behind an env var or request-level param
- Default behavior (no flag) runs strategy A and is unchanged for existing callers
- All three strategies use the same `CatalogSearchService` with the same gender/price filters
- Strategy B makes exactly one Haiku/Sonnet call per category to generate the HyDE description
- Strategy C pulls top 30 per category then runs one Haiku rerank call per category
- Unit tests cover strategy routing and confirm strategy A output matches current baseline

input.content:
Run the retrieval bake-off eval using the three strategies from the companion sub-task and commit results to `backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/`.

**Eval setup:**
- Select ~20 representative vibes covering: celebrity-only, celebrity+photo, different aesthetics, both genders
- For each vibe, capture top 5 items per category from strategies A, B, and C
- LLM judge: one Claude Sonnet call per (vibe × strategy × category) — sees the VibeProfile and 5 items (descriptions + image URLs); returns a 1–10 score, one-line rationale, and flagged-issue list
- Human spot-check: eyeball ~5 of the 20 vibes end-to-end to sanity-check judge consistency

**Report output** (`backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/report.md`):
- Per-vibe winners table (which strategy won each category)
- Aggregate mean judge score per strategy
- Per-category score breakdown
- ≥3 side-by-side comparisons (vibe → A items vs B items vs C items)
- Written recommendation: which strategy to productionize and what the next retrieval ticket should be

Raw LLM judge outputs saved to `backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/raw.jsonl`.

## Context

This eval is the decision gate before productionizing any retrieval strategy. The current sparse-query / dense-catalog mismatch is a likely ceiling on outfit quality; the eval proves which fix actually wins on real vibes before engineering time is committed.

## Acceptance criteria

- Eval runs end-to-end on ~20 representative vibes without manual intervention
- LLM judge scores committed in `backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/raw.jsonl` (machine-readable)
- `backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/report.md` present with per-vibe winners, aggregate scores, per-category breakdown, and ≥3 side-by-side comparisons
- Human spot-check of ~5 vibes documented in the report
- Written recommendation committed with the report naming the strategy to productionize and the next retrieval ticket

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Create two subtasks under KAN-74 via Jira REST API."""
import base64, json, os, sys, urllib.request, urllib.error

server = "https://jaeyoonsworkspace-23358853.atlassian.net"
login  = "jaeyoonbae99@gmail.com"
token  = os.environ["JIRA_API_TOKEN"]
creds  = base64.b64encode(f"{login}:{token}".encode()).decode()

HEADERS = {
    "Authorization": f"Basic {creds}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "Accept": "application/json",
}

ST1_SUMMARY = "Implement retrieval strategies A/B/C behind a feature flag in find-pieces"
ST1_BODY = (
    "Add retrieval strategies A, B (HyDE), and C (HyDE + rerank) side-by-side behind a "
    "feature flag in `backend/src/find-pieces/`. All three strategies use the same "
    "`CatalogSearchService` with the same gender/price filters.\n\n"
    "**Strategy A (control):** existing behavior — query string from anchor + tags + color names, "
    "one `text-embedding-3-small` embedding, one cosine search per category. Wrap behind the flag "
    "without changing default behavior.\n\n"
    "**Strategy B (HyDE):** for each category (top/bottom/dress/shoes/accessory), one Haiku/Sonnet "
    "call writes an ideal product description in catalog prose voice. Embed that description and run "
    "the cosine search.\n\n"
    "**Strategy C (HyDE + rerank):** same as B, but pull top 30 per category from vector search, "
    "then run a Haiku reranker pass scoring each candidate against the full VibeProfile, returning "
    "top 5 with a one-line rationale.\n\n"
    "## Acceptance criteria\n\n"
    "- Strategy A/B/C implemented in `backend/src/find-pieces/` behind an env var or request-level param\n"
    "- Default (no flag) runs strategy A, preserving existing behavior for all callers\n"
    "- All three strategies use the same `CatalogSearchService` with the same gender/price filters\n"
    "- Strategy B makes exactly one Haiku/Sonnet call per category to generate the HyDE description\n"
    "- Strategy C pulls top 30 per category then runs one Haiku rerank call per category\n"
    "- Unit tests cover strategy routing and confirm strategy A output matches current baseline"
)

ST2_SUMMARY = "Run retrieval bake-off eval on 20 vibes and commit report with recommendation"
ST2_BODY = (
    "Run the retrieval bake-off eval using the three strategies from the companion sub-task "
    "and commit results under `backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/`.\n\n"
    "**Eval setup:**\n"
    "- Select ~20 representative vibes covering: celebrity-only, celebrity+photo, different aesthetics, "
    "both genders\n"
    "- For each vibe, capture top 5 items per category from strategies A, B, and C\n"
    "- LLM judge: one Claude Sonnet call per (vibe x strategy x category) — sees the VibeProfile and "
    "5 items (descriptions + image URLs); returns a 1-10 score, one-line rationale, and flagged issues\n"
    "- Human spot-check: eyeball ~5 of the 20 vibes end-to-end to sanity-check judge consistency\n\n"
    "**Report** (`backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/report.md`):\n"
    "- Per-vibe winners table (which strategy won each category)\n"
    "- Aggregate mean judge score per strategy\n"
    "- Per-category score breakdown\n"
    "- 3+ side-by-side comparisons (vibe -> A items vs B items vs C items)\n"
    "- Written recommendation: which strategy to productionize and what the next retrieval ticket should be\n\n"
    "Raw LLM judge outputs saved to `backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/raw.jsonl`.\n\n"
    "## Context\n\n"
    "This eval is the decision gate before productionizing any retrieval strategy. The current "
    "sparse-query / dense-catalog mismatch is a likely ceiling on outfit quality.\n\n"
    "## Acceptance criteria\n\n"
    "- Eval runs end-to-end on ~20 representative vibes without manual intervention\n"
    "- LLM judge scores committed in `backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/raw.jsonl` (machine-readable)\n"
    "- `backend/data/retrieval-bakeoff/report.md` present with per-vibe winners, aggregate scores, "
    "per-category breakdown, and 3+ side-by-side comparisons\n"
    "- Human spot-check of ~5 vibes documented in the report\n"
    "- Written recommendation committed with the report naming the strategy to productionize and the "
    "next retrieval ticket"
)


def post_issue(summary, body, parent_key):
    payload = {
        "fields": {
            "project": {"key": "KAN"},
            "issuetype": {"id": "10002"},   # Subtask
            "parent": {"key": parent_key},
            "summary": summary,
            "description": {
                "type": "doc",
                "version": 1,
                "content": [
                    {
                        "type": "paragraph",
                        "content": [{"type": "text", "text": body}]
                    }
                ]
            }
        }
    }
    data = json.dumps(payload).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"{server}/rest/api/3/issue",
        data=data,
        headers=HEADERS,
        method="POST"
    )
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as r:
            result = json.loads(r.read())
            return result["key"]
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        msg = e.read().decode()
        print(f"HTTP {e.code}: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)


def link_issues(blocker, blocked):
    """blocker Blocks blocked."""
    payload = {
        "type": {"name": "Blocks"},
        "inwardIssue":  {"key": blocked},
        "outwardIssue": {"key": blocker},
    }
    data = json.dumps(payload).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"{server}/rest/api/3/issueLink",
        data=data,
        headers=HEADERS,
        method="POST"
    )
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as r:
            pass  # 201 No Content
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        if e.code == 201:
            return
        msg = e.read().decode()
        print(f"Link HTTP {e.code}: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)


st1 = post_issue(ST1_SUMMARY, ST1_BODY, "KAN-74")
print(f"Created ST1: {st1}")

st2 = post_issue(ST2_SUMMARY, ST2_BODY, "KAN-74")
print(f"Created ST2: {st2}")

link_issues(st1, st2)
print(f"Linked: {st1} Blocks {st2}")

                    }
                ]
            }
        }
    }
    data = json.dumps(payload).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"{server}/rest/api/3/issue",
        data=data,
        headers=HEADERS,
        method="POST"
    )
    try:
